# Awesome Agent Platforms [![Awesome](https://awesome.re/badge-flat2.svg)](https://awesome.re)

[![Live site](https://img.shields.io/badge/live-agents.jrdevelopr.site-2563eb?logo=googlechrome&logoColor=white)](https://agents.jrdevelopr.site) [![Platforms](https://img.shields.io/badge/platforms-27-0e9f6e)](https://agents.jrdevelopr.site) [![Auto-updated weekly](https://img.shields.io/badge/auto--updated-weekly-7c3aed)](bin/discover.sh) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue)](LICENSE)

> An **alphabetical, self-hosted-first** comparison of AI coding-agent platforms — find the best tool to **run and manage AI coding agents** from one place.

### ▶︎ Live, interactive version → **https://agents.jrdevelopr.site**

The goal: **one tool that does everything** — open an interactive shell, pull a GitHub issue, run any
agent CLI, and ship a PR, without tab-hopping to a separate terminal.

**Listed alphabetically** (A→Z) — no personal ranking. **Compared on:** **self-hosted** (runs on *your*
machines) · one-click **human shell** · **GitHub** issue sync + PRs/push · **many agents/models** (beyond
Claude & Codex) · **multiple logins** (separate client subscriptions) · **mobile** access · GitHub stars &
user sentiment. Free & self-hosted preferred; paid options are included and clearly marked.

**Legend:** ✅ native / strong · 🟡 partial / context-only · ➖ not documented &nbsp;|&nbsp;
*Self-hosted* ✅ = runs entirely on your own machines (not the vendor's cloud). *Multiple logins* =
isolate separate Claude/agent subscriptions per project. *Mobile* ✅ = app or mobile-first.

| # | Platform | ★ Stars | Cost | Self-hosted | GitHub Issues | Projects | PRs / push | One-click shell | Multiple logins | Mobile | Agents / models | Bottom line (what users say) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **[AgentOS](https://github.com/saadnvd1/agent-os)** | 158 | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Mobile-first web (PWA) — designed for phones</sub> | Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Kilo, Gemini, Aider, Cursor, Amp, Pi + more | Niche mobile-first self-hosted UI with multi-pane terminals and broad agent support (Claude/Codex/Aider/Gemini). Low visibility — little community feedback yet. |
| 2 | **[Agentrove](https://github.com/Mng-dev-ai/agentrove)** | 290 | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive); desktop app too</sub> | Claude, Codex, Copilot, Cursor, OpenCode | Ambitious all-in-one workspace (chat, editor, terminal, diffs, git) running many agents in Docker/host sandboxes. Explicitly pre-1.0 — maintainers warn of breaking changes; small user base. |
| 3 | **[AgentsMesh](https://agentsmesh.ai)** | 2.2k | **Freemium** <br><sub>BSL-1.1 · self-host · BYOK cloud</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Native iOS (SwiftUI) client + web / tablet</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, Aider, OpenCode + custom (MCP) | Fleet console to schedule and isolate hundreds of agents across your own machines, with web/desktop/iOS clients. Powerful but involved self-host setup; little organic discussion yet. |
| 4 | **[Claude Plane](https://github.com/kodrunhq/claude-plane)** | 1 | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Mobile browser terminal</sub> | Claude CLI only | Self-hosted control plane for distributed Claude CLI sessions (browser shells, gRPC agents, Docker-first). Brand new and Claude-only — no users or reviews yet. |
| 5 | **[CloudCLI](https://github.com/siteboon/claudecodeui)** | 12k | Free <br><sub>GPL-3</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Mobile-optimized web UI (use on the go)</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, OpenCode | Popular free web/mobile UI for Claude Code & other CLIs — praised for responsive remote-from-phone use, terminal, files & git. Gripe: heavy open-issue churn and remote-setup/security friction. |
| 6 | **[cmux](https://github.com/manaflow-ai/cmux)** | 23k | **Freemium** <br><sub>GPL-3 · paid Founder tier</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ➖ | 🟡<br><sub>Early-access iOS app; macOS desktop</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, Grok, Cursor, Copilot, Amp, OpenCode, Pi + more | Breakout macOS hit (~22k★) — fast Ghostty/GPU terminal with sidebar git/PR status and the widest agent roster; reception strongly positive. Gripe: macOS-only, GPL-3, niche to heavy parallel-agent use. |
| 7 | **[Coder](https://coder.com)** | 14k | **Freemium** <br><sub>free core · Premium $</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | ✅ | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive); no native app</sub> | Terminal/MCP agents, built-in modules, custom agents | Established, well-regarded self-hosted dev-environment platform now leaning into agent infra — strong privacy/control for enterprises. Gripe: heavy Terraform/K8s setup; user management gated behind paid Premium. |
| 8 | **[Conductor](https://conductor.build)** | — | Free <br><sub>proprietary · BYO Claude/Codex</sub> | ✅ | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖ | ➖<br><sub>macOS desktop app only</sub> | Claude Code, Codex, Cursor (+ Bedrock / Vertex / OpenRouter) | One of the most polished macOS parallel-agent runners — clean worktree workspaces, easy diff review, uses your existing Claude login. Gripe: closed-source, Mac-only; early flap over broad GitHub permissions (since fixed). |
| 9 | **[Cursor](https://cursor.com)** | — | 💲 **Paid** <br><sub>Pro $20/mo + usage</sub> | ➖ | 🟡 | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡<br><sub>Mobile web for background agents</sub> | Cursor models + some BYOK | The dominant AI IDE of 2026 — best-in-class autocomplete, strong agent mode and parallel cloud agents. Gripe: a 2025 usage-based pricing change angered users (CEO apologized) and agents sometimes overreach. |
| 10 | **[Devin](https://devin.ai)** | — | 💲 **Paid** <br><sub>from ~$20/mo (teams $)</sub> | ➖ | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡<br><sub>Web + Slack (drive from phone)</sub> | Devin’s own proprietary agent | First "autonomous AI engineer" — real ROI on high-volume, well-scoped tasks with end-to-end PRs. Gripe: over-promised vs reality, pricey (~$500/mo), and loops on ambiguous or novel work. |
| 11 | **[Emdash](https://emdash.sh)** | 4.9k | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>Desktop app only (Mac/Win/Linux)</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode, Kilo + many more | Fast-rising YC W26 open-source parallel-agent IDE — users love provider-agnostic parallel worktrees and clean diff review. Gripe: desktop-only Electron (no server mode) and very young. |
| 12 | **[Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent)** | 198k | Free <br><sub>Nous Research</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Drive via Telegram / WhatsApp / Slack + web dashboard</sub> | Model-agnostic (OpenRouter or direct: Claude, GPT, local…) | One of the fastest-growing agent repos — its markdown "skills" self-improving memory genuinely impresses. Gripe: polarized by plagiarism/attribution claims and heavy-handed issue moderation; messaging-gateway focus, not a shell fleet. |
| 13 | **[Jean](https://github.com/coollabsio/jean)** | 1.1k | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖ | 🟡<br><sub>Headless web access over HTTP (mobile browser)</sub> | Claude, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode + MCP | New worktree-isolated desktop runner for Claude/Codex/Cursor/OpenCode from the well-regarded Coolify team. Very new and overlaps Conductor/cmux — no real user feedback yet. |
| 14 | **[Kilo.ai](https://kilo.ai)** | 23k | **Freemium** <br><sub>BYOK free · cloud paid</sub> | 🟡 | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡<br><sub>Cloud web console in mobile browser</sub> | Hundreds of models, BYOK + local | Well-received open-source agentic IDE — 500+ models, zero-markup BYOK/local pricing, "Cursor but open & free" (~1.5M users claimed). Gripe: steeper setup, variable API costs, strict no-refund policy. |
| 15 | **[Multica](https://multica.ai)** | 37k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive)</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, Copilot, OpenCode, Kiro, Kimi + more | High-star OSS "agents as teammates" fleet manager — auto-detects many CLIs, vendor-neutral, self-hostable. Gripe: manual skill curation and limited runtimes; independent user discussion still thin. |
| 16 | **[mux](https://github.com/coder/mux)** | 1.9k | Free <br><sub>AGPL-3</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡<br><sub>Server mode = responsive mobile web</sub> | Claude, GPT / Codex, Grok, Ollama, OpenRouter models | Polished Coder-built desktop app for isolated parallel agents (worktrees/SSH, multi-model). Gripe: young, fast-moving v0.x (AGPL), Electron desktop-only, still maturing. |
| 17 | **[Netclode](https://github.com/angristan/netclode)** | 160 | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>SSH client only (Tailscale)</sub> | Any harness — Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Copilot | Admired solo-dev showcase — self-hosted k3s + Kata microVM + Tailscale stack with a slick iOS app. Gripe: heavy, complex infra for one user; niche and single-maintainer. |
| 18 | **[Omnara](https://github.com/omnara-ai/omnara)** | 2.6k | **Freemium** <br><sub>legacy archived</sub> | 🟡 | ➖ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>iOS + Android app (monitor/control from phone)</sub> | Claude-centric (legacy: Claude/Codex) | Positively-received YC S25 tool — run/monitor Claude Code & Codex from web & mobile, answer prompts from your phone, easy pip install. Gripe: young, limited free tier, mobile/frontend not fully open at launch. |
| 19 | **[OpenASE](https://github.com/PacificStudio/openase)** | 254 | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ✅ | ➖<br><sub>Mobile / desktop apps on roadmap; web only now</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini CLI | New ticket-driven orchestration — Kanban ticket-to-code with multi-agent workflows and full traceability, self-hostable. Very new and unproven; small community. |
| 20 | **[OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw)** | 380k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Via chat channels (Slack/Telegram/…)</sub> | Model-agnostic; thousands of pre-built tools/workflows | Explosive 2026 breakout (~380k★) — self-hosted personal assistant plugging into WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord via a huge connector ecosystem. Gripe: can rack up large API costs fast; chaotic rename history. |
| 21 | **[OpenCode](https://opencode.ai)** | 177k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Mobile browser (web UI) / terminal</sub> | Any of 75+ providers incl. free & local models | Very popular open-source Claude Code alternative — model freedom (75+ providers incl. local) and a better terminal, often a cheaper daily driver. Gripe: recurring TUI/terminal bugs across platforms. |
| 22 | **[Paperclip](https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip)** | 71k | Free <br><sub>MIT</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive) control plane</sub> | Any agent/provider/runtime via adapters | Viral "AI company / org-chart" orchestrator — slick all-in-one dashboard of agents, goals, budgets and cost tracking. Gripe: polarizing — critics call the zero-human-company premise a gimmick with no quality control. |
| 23 | **[Paseo](https://github.com/getpaseo/paseo)** | 8.9k | Free <br><sub>open-source</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ➖ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Mobile client (iOS/Android) + desktop/web/CLI</sub> | Dozens of coding-agent CLIs | Strong HN reception as a privacy-first multi-device orchestrator — phone control called "life-changing", no telemetry, multi-provider, voice mode. Gripe: "beautiful" marketing disputed; users want cost tracking to curb token waste. |
| 24 | **[RCFlow](https://www.rcflow.app)** | 1 | Free <br><sub>AGPL</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | ➖ | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅<br><sub>Mobile client (+ desktop)</sub> | Claude, Codex, OpenCode (Anthropic/Bedrock/OpenAI-compat) | Very new coding-agent backend pitching split-view multi-agent sessions, low-lag WebSockets and session persistence. Unproven — no community footprint yet. |
| 25 | **[Sortie](https://github.com/sortie-ai/sortie)** | 82 | Free <br><sub>Apache-2.0</sub> | ✅ | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡 | ➖ | ➖ | ➖<br><sub>Headless daemon — no UI</sub> | Claude, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode, Kiro | Niche headless issue-to-agent orchestrator — single Go binary, tracker-agnostic, isolated workspaces with retries and cost tracking; honest that it only orchestrates. Tiny adoption, little discussion. |
| 26 | **[SwarmClaw](https://swarmclaw.ai)** | 587 | Free <br><sub>MIT</sub> | ✅ | ➖ | ➖ | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Mobile-friendly responsive web</sub> | Claude, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot, Cursor, Qwen, Goose + 24 providers | Early OSS multi-agent runtime — broad provider support (23+ LLMs), MCP tools, memory and scheduling, easy npm install. Obscure and unproven; almost no real-world reviews. |
| 27 | **[Vibe Kanban](https://github.com/BloopAI/vibe-kanban)** | 27k | Free <br><sub>open-source · sunsetting</sub> | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | ✅ | 🟡 | 🟡<br><sub>Web (responsive)</sub> | Claude, Codex, Gemini, Copilot, Cursor, OpenCode, Qwen + | Well-liked kanban over Claude Code/Codex/Gemini with isolated worktrees for parallel work. Major caveat: BloopAI is sunsetting it — future rests on community maintenance. |

---

### How this stays current
The table is generated from the data in **[`site/index.html`](site/index.html)** (a single `PLATFORMS`
array). A weekly bot scans curated lists of new agent platforms, adds any that are missing, re-ranks,
and regenerates both the live site and this README — so they never drift.

**Suggest a platform:** [open an issue](https://github.com/jrdevelopr/awesome-agent-platforms/issues/new?title=Suggest%20a%20platform:%20).

<sub>Ratings are based on official docs/repos; “➖”/“🟡” are used instead of guessing. Projects = the platform's own
project/task board or PM-tool integration (Linear, Jira, GitHub Projects). Prices are list-price estimates and change often — verify before buying.
This is a personal, independent comparison, free to share.</sub>
